context.pr_title: fix: no-play argument for API client
context.pr_body:
**Is this PR adding new feature or fix a BUG?**

Fix BUG.

**Is this pull request related to any issue? If yes, please link the issue.**

N/A

**Description**

Currently, `--play` has no effect because its default value is `True`. The parser does not recognize anything like `--play False`. It only detect whether the argument exists.

Therefore, we can use [BooleanOptionalAction](https://docs.python.org/3/library/argparse.html#argparse.BooleanOptionalAction), which automatically add the `--no-play` option, so that the play argument can be set to `False`.

**Compatibility**

This change does not break the existing behaviors because it adds a new option.

agents.system_prompt:
You are an Analysis Agent specialized in Pull Request analysis. ## Project Context (MASCA Analysis) ## Project Analysis ### README

Fish Speech

**English** | [简体中文](docs/README.zh.md) | [Portuguese](docs/README.pt-BR.md) | [日本語](docs/README.ja.md) | [한국어](docs/README.ko.md)




This codebase is released under Apache License and all model weights are released under CC-BY-NC-SA-4.0 License. Please refer to [LICENSE](LICENSE) for more details. --- ## Fish Agent We are very excited to announce that we have made our self-research agent demo open source, you can now try our agent demo online at [demo](https://fish.audio/demo/live) for instant English chat and English and Chinese chat locally by following the [docs](https://speech.fish.audio/start_agent/). You should mention that the content is released under a **CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 licence**. And the demo is an early alpha test version, the inference speed needs to be optimised, and there are a lot of bugs waiting to be fixed. If you've found a bug or want to fix it, we'd be very happy to receive an issue or a pull request. ## Features ### Fish Speech 1. **Zero-shot & Few-shot TTS:** Input a 10 to 30-second vocal sample to generate high-quality TTS output. **For detailed guidelines, see [Voice Cloning Best Practices](https://docs.fish.audio/text-to-speech/voice-clone-best-practices).** 2. **Multilingual & Cross-lingual Suppor ## Your Task When you receive a PR title and body, you must: 1. **Analyze the request**: Understand what the PR wants to achieve 2. **Explore the code**: Use tools to navigate base_project and find relevant files/functions 3. **Identify targets**: Determine exactly which files and functions need to be modified 4. **Optimize MASCA**: Produce a version of the MASCA analysis focused on the specific PR ## Available Tools - `read_base_project_file(file_path)`: Read a file from the project - `list_base_project_directory(directory, pattern, recursive)`: List files/folders ## Required Output You must produce a structured output with: - `pr_title`: PR title - `pr_body`: PR body - `masca_optimized`: MASCA optimized for this specific PR - `files_to_modify`: List of files to modify with motivation - `functions_to_modify`: List of functions to modify with motivation - `analysis_summary`: Summary of your analysis ## Guidelines - Explore the code before drawing conclusions - Search for files with names relevant to the PR - Identify specific functions, not just generic files - Be precise with paths (relative to base_project) - For function names use the format "ClassName.method_name" for methods
agents.input_prompt:
Analyze this Pull Request:

## Title
fix: no-play argument for API client

## Description
**Is this PR adding new feature or fix a BUG?**

Fix BUG.

**Is this pull request related to any issue? If yes, please link the issue.**

N/A

**Description**

Currently, `--play` has no effect because its default value is `True`. The parser does not recognize anything like `--play False`. It only detect whether the argument exists.

Therefore, we can use [BooleanOptionalAction](https://docs.python.org/3/library/argparse.html#argparse.BooleanOptionalAction), which automatically add the `--no-play` option, so that the play argument can be set to `False`.

**Compatibility**

This change does not break the existing behaviors because it adds a new option.

Explore the source code to identify the files and functions to modify.

arguments.reason: PR mentions API client --play flag; need to inspect argparse setup and play logic.
arguments.expected_information: Where --play is defined, how args are parsed, and where playback is triggered.

tool_calls.raw_result:
import argparse
import base64
import wave

import ormsgpack
import pyaudio
import requests
from pydub import AudioSegment
from pydub.playback import play

from tools.file import audio_to_bytes, read_ref_text
from tools.schema import ServeReferenceAudio, ServeTTSRequest


def parse_args():

    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
        description="Send a WAV file and text to a server and receive synthesized audio.",
        formatter_class=argparse.RawTextHelpFormatter,
    )

    parser.add_argument(
        "--url",
        "-u",
        type=str,
        default="http://127.0.0.1:8080/v1/tts",
        help="URL of the server",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--text", "-t", type=str, required=True, help="Text to be synthesized"
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--reference_id",
        "-id",
        type=str,
        default=None,
        help="ID of the reference model to be used for the speech\n(Local: name of folder containing audios and files)",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--reference_audio",
        "-ra",
        type=str,
        nargs="+",
        default=None,
        help="Path to the audio file",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--reference_text",
        "-rt",
        type=str,
        nargs="+",
        default=None,
        help="Reference text for voice synthesis",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--output",
        "-o",
        type=str,
        default="generated_audio",
        help="Output audio file name",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--play",
        type=bool,
        default=True,
        help="Whether to play audio after receiving data",
    )
    parser.add_argument("--normalize", type=bool, default=True)
    parser.add_argument(
        "--format", type=str, choices=["wav", "mp3", "flac"], default="wav"
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--latency",
        type=str,
        default="normal",
        choices=["normal", "balanced"],
        help="Used in api.fish.audio/v1/tts",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--max_new_tokens",
        type=int,
        default=1024,
        help="Maximum new tokens to generate. \n0 means no limit.",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--chunk_length", type=int, default=200, help="Chunk length for synthesis"
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--top_p", type=float, default=0.7, help="Top-p sampling for synthesis"
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--repetition_penalty",
        type=float,
        default=1.2,
        help="Repetition penalty for synthesis",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--temperature", type=float, default=0.7, help="Temperature for sampling"
    )

    parser.add_argument(
        "--streaming", type=bool, default=False, help="Enable streaming response"
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--channels", type=int, default=1, help="Number of audio channels"
    )
    parser.add_argument("--rate", type=int, default=44100, help="Sample rate for audio")
    parser.add_argument(
        "--use_memory_cache",
        type=str,
        default="off",
        choices=["on", "off"],
        help="Cache encoded references codes in memory.\n",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--seed",
        type=int,
        default=None,
        help="`None` means randomized inference, otherwise deterministic.\n"
        "It can't be used for fixing a timbre.",
    )

    return parser.parse_args()


if __name__ == "__main__":

    args = parse_args()

    idstr: str | None = args.reference_id
    # priority: ref_id > [{text, audio},...]
    if idstr is None:
        ref_audios = args.reference_audio
        ref_texts = args.reference_text
        if ref_audios is None:
            byte_audios = []
        else:
            byte_audios = [audio_to_bytes(ref_audio) for ref_audio in ref_audios]
        if ref_texts is None:
            ref_texts = []
        else:
            ref_texts = [read_ref_text(ref_text) for ref_text in ref_texts]
    else:
        byte_audios = []
        ref_texts = []
        pass  # in api.py

    data = {
        "text": args.text,
        "references": [
            ServeReferenceAudio(
                audio=ref_audio if ref_audio is not None else b"", text=ref_text
            )
            for ref_text, ref_audio in zip(ref_texts, byte_audios)
        ],
        "reference_id": idstr,
        "normalize": args.normalize,
        "format": args.format,
        "max_new_tokens": args.max_new_tokens,
        "chunk_length": args.chunk_length,
        "top_p": args.top_p,
        "repetition_penalty": args.repetition_penalty,
        "temperature": args.temperature,
        "streaming": args.streaming,
        "use_memory_cache": args.use_memory_cache,
        "seed": args.seed,
    }

    pydantic_data = ServeTTSRequest(**data)

    response = requests.post(
        args.url,
        data=ormsgpack.packb(pydantic_data, option=ormsgpack.OPT_SERIALIZE_PYDANTIC),
        stream=args.streaming,
        headers={
            "authorization": "Bearer YOUR_API_KEY",
            "content-type": "application/msgpack",
        },
    )

    if response.status_code == 200:
        if args.streaming:
            p = pyaudio.PyAudio()
            audio_format = pyaudio.paInt16  # Assuming 16-bit PCM format
            stream = p.open(
                format=audio_format, channels=args.channels, rate=args.rate, output=True
            )

            wf = wave.open(f"{args.output}.wav", "wb")
            wf.setnchannels(args.channels)
            wf.setsampwidth(p.get_sample_size(audio_format))
            wf.setframerate(args.rate)

            stream_stopped_flag = False

            try:
                for chunk in response.iter_content(chunk_size=1024):
                    if chunk:
                        stream.write(chunk)
                        wf.writeframesraw(chunk)
                    else:
                        if not stream_stopped_flag:
                            stream.stop_stream()
                            stream_stopped_flag = True
            finally:
                stream.close()
                p.terminate()
                wf.close()
        else:
            audio_content = response.content
            audio_path = f"{args.output}.{args.format}"
            with open(audio_path, "wb") as audio_file:
                audio_file.write(audio_content)

            audio = AudioSegment.from_file(audio_path, format=args.format)
            if args.play:
                play(audio)
            print(f"Audio has been saved to '{audio_path}'.")
    else:
        print(f"Request failed with status code {response.status_code}")
        print(response.json())
tool_calls.result:
### File Overview
`tools/api_client.py` is a CLI script that posts a TTS request (text + optional reference audio/text or reference_id) to a server endpoint and then saves the returned audio; it can optionally play the audio locally via a `--play` flag.

### Architecture
- **CLI layer (`parse_args`)**: Defines all command-line arguments via `argparse` and returns the parsed namespace.
- **Main execution block (`if __name__ == "__main__":`)**:
  1. Parses args.
  2. Builds reference inputs either from `--reference_id` or from paired `--reference_audio`/`--reference_text` files (converted via helper functions).
  3. Constructs a `ServeTTSRequest` Pydantic model (from `tools.schema`) and serializes it as MessagePack with `ormsgpack`.
  4. Sends a POST request via `requests.post(..., stream=args.streaming)`.
  5. Handles the response in two modes:
     - **Streaming**: plays raw PCM chunks in real time using `pyaudio` and writes a `.wav` file via `wave`.
     - **Non-streaming**: writes the full audio file, optionally plays it via `pydub.playback.play`.

### Functions & Classes
- `parse_args` — Builds and returns the `argparse.ArgumentParser` result.  
  **Importance**: This is where `--play` is defined and how its value is parsed/typed; it also defines `--streaming`, output naming, audio format, and other synthesis parameters that flow into the request payload.

- `ServeReferenceAudio` (imported from `tools.schema`) — Used to package each reference pair (`audio`, `text`) into the `references` list in the request.  
  **Importance**: Determines how local reference audio/text inputs are represented in the API request.

- `ServeTTSRequest` (imported from `tools.schema`) — Pydantic request model instantiated as `ServeTTSRequest(**data)` before serialization.  
  **Importance**: Central contract for what fields are sent to the server; args are mapped into this structure.

- `audio_to_bytes` (imported from `tools.file`) — Converts a reference audio file path to raw bytes.  
  **Importance**: Enables sending reference audio content inline when `--reference_id` is not used.

- `read_ref_text` (imported from `tools.file`) — Reads reference text from a file path (as implied by name/usage).  
  **Importance**: Enables sending reference text content inline; used alongside `audio_to_bytes`.

- Main block `if __name__ == "__main__":` — Orchestrates end-to-end behavior: argument parsing, request building, HTTP call, audio saving, and playback.  
  **Importance**: Contains the **actual playback trigger** and the **streaming playback logic**.

  Key internal logic relevant to playback:
  - Non-streaming playback path:
    - `audio = AudioSegment.from_file(audio_path, format=args.format)`
    - `if args.play: play(audio)` — **This is where playback is triggered** when not streaming.
  - Streaming playback path:
    - Creates a `pyaudio.PyAudio()` output stream and writes each `chunk` from `response.iter_content(...)` to `stream.write(chunk)` — effectively always playing streaming audio when `args.streaming` is enabled (no `args.play` gate here).
    - Simultaneously writes to a `.wav` file using `wave.open(..., "wb")` and `wf.writeframesraw(chunk)`.

### Relevance to Agent's Goal
- **Where `--play` is defined**: in `parse_args`:
  - `parser.add_argument("--play", type=bool, default=True, help="Whether to play audio after receiving data")`
- **How args are parsed**: `args = parse_args()` in the main block; note that `--play` is parsed as `type=bool`, which in `argparse` commonly leads to surprising behavior (e.g., `--play False` may still evaluate truthy depending on how it’s provided), and there is no `store_true/store_false` pattern.
- **Where playback is triggered**:
  - Non-streaming: `if args.play: play(audio)`
  - Streaming: playback happens unconditionally via `pyaudio` when `args.streaming` is `True` (no check of `args.play`), so `--play` currently does **not** control streaming playback.
- If the PR is about a “API client `--play` flag”, the most relevant code to modify is:
  1. The `--play` argument definition in `parse_args` (its `type=bool` behavior).
  2. The streaming branch to optionally respect `args.play` (or to split “play” vs “save” concerns).

You are a code analysis assistant. Your task is to summarize source code files for an AI agent that is planning code modifications for a Pull Request.

Your summary MUST:
1. Preserve ALL exact function names, class names, and method names exactly as they appear in the source code — never rename or abbreviate them.
2. Explain the overall architecture and purpose of the file.
3. Describe the role and importance of each function, class, and method.
4. Highlight the parts most relevant to the agent's stated goal.

Format your response with these sections:
### File Overview
[1-2 sentences on the file's purpose]

### Architecture
[How the main components are organised and interact]

### Functions & Classes
[For each function/class/method: `exact_name` — description and its importance]

### Relevance to Agent's Goal
[How this file relates to the agent's reason for reading it]

            byte_audios = []
        else:
            byte_audios = [audio_to_bytes(ref_audio) for ref_audio in ref_audios]
        if ref_texts is None:
            ref_texts = []
        else:
            ref_texts = [read_ref_text(ref_text) for ref_text in ref_texts]
    else:
        byte_audios = []
        ref_texts = []
        pass  # in api.py

    data = {
        "text": args.text,
        "references": [
            ServeReferenceAudio(
                audio=ref_audio if ref_audio is not None else b"", text=ref_text
            )
            for ref_text, ref_audio in zip(ref_texts, byte_audios)
        ],
        "reference_id": idstr,
        "normalize": args.normalize,
        "format": args.format,
        "max_new_tokens": args.max_new_tokens,
        "chunk_length": args.chunk_length,
        "top_p": args.top_p,
        "repetition_penalty": args.repetition_penalty,
        "temperature": args.temperature,
        "streaming": args.streaming,
        "use_memory_cache": args.use_memory_cache,
        "seed": args.seed,
    }

    pydantic_data = ServeTTSRequest(**data)

    response = requests.post(
        args.url,
        data=ormsgpack.packb(pydantic_data, option=ormsgpack.OPT_SERIALIZE_PYDANTIC),
        stream=args.streaming,
        headers={
            "authorization": "Bearer YOUR_API_KEY",
            "content-type": "application/msgpack",
        },
    )

    if response.status_code == 200:
        if args.streaming:
            p = pyaudio.PyAudio()
            audio_format = pyaudio.paInt16  # Assuming 16-bit PCM format
            stream = p.open(
                format=audio_format, channels=args.channels, rate=args.rate, output=True
            )

            wf = wave.open(f"{args.output}.wav", "wb")
            wf.setnchannels(args.channels)
            wf.setsampwidth(p.get_sample_size(audio_format))
            wf.setframerate(args.rate)

            stream_stopped_flag = False

            try:
                for chunk in response.iter_content(chunk_size=1024):
                    if chunk:
                        stream.write(chunk)
                        wf.writeframesraw(chunk)
                    else:
                        if not stream_stopped_flag:
                            stream.stop_stream()
                            stream_stopped_flag = True
            finally:
                stream.close()
                p.terminate()
                wf.close()
        else:
            audio_content = response.content
            audio_path = f"{args.output}.{args.format}"
            with open(audio_path, "wb") as audio_file:
                audio_file.write(audio_content)

            audio = AudioSegment.from_file(audio_path, format=args.format)
            if args.play:
                play(audio)
            print(f"Audio has been saved to '{audio_path}'.")
    else:
        print(f"Request failed with status code {response.status_code}")
        print(response.json())
summarization.summary:
### File Overview
`tools/api_client.py` is a CLI script that posts a TTS request (text + optional reference audio/text or reference_id) to a server endpoint and then saves the returned audio; it can optionally play the audio locally via a `--play` flag.

### Architecture
- **CLI layer (`parse_args`)**: Defines all command-line arguments via `argparse` and returns the parsed namespace.
- **Main execution block (`if __name__ == "__main__":`)**:
  1. Parses args.
  2. Builds reference inputs either from `--reference_id` or from paired `--reference_audio`/`--reference_text` files (converted via helper functions).
  3. Constructs a `ServeTTSRequest` Pydantic model (from `tools.schema`) and serializes it as MessagePack with `ormsgpack`.
  4. Sends a POST request via `requests.post(..., stream=args.streaming)`.
  5. Handles the response in two modes:
     - **Streaming**: plays raw PCM chunks in real time using `pyaudio` and writes a `.wav` file via `wave`.
     - **Non-streaming**: writes the full audio file, optionally plays it via `pydub.playback.play`.

### Functions & Classes
- `parse_args` — Builds and returns the `argparse.ArgumentParser` result.  
  **Importance**: This is where `--play` is defined and how its value is parsed/typed; it also defines `--streaming`, output naming, audio format, and other synthesis parameters that flow into the request payload.

- `ServeReferenceAudio` (imported from `tools.schema`) — Used to package each reference pair (`audio`, `text`) into the `references` list in the request.  
  **Importance**: Determines how local reference audio/text inputs are represented in the API request.

- `ServeTTSRequest` (imported from `tools.schema`) — Pydantic request model instantiated as `ServeTTSRequest(**data)` before serialization.  
  **Importance**: Central contract for what fields are sent to the server; args are mapped into this structure.

- `audio_to_bytes` (imported from `tools.file`) — Converts a reference audio file path to raw bytes.  
  **Importance**: Enables sending reference audio content inline when `--reference_id` is not used.

- `read_ref_text` (imported from `tools.file`) — Reads reference text from a file path (as implied by name/usage).  
  **Importance**: Enables sending reference text content inline; used alongside `audio_to_bytes`.

- Main block `if __name__ == "__main__":` — Orchestrates end-to-end behavior: argument parsing, request building, HTTP call, audio saving, and playback.  
  **Importance**: Contains the **actual playback trigger** and the **streaming playback logic**.

  Key internal logic relevant to playback:
  - Non-streaming playback path:
    - `audio = AudioSegment.from_file(audio_path, format=args.format)`
    - `if args.play: play(audio)` — **This is where playback is triggered** when not streaming.
  - Streaming playback path:
    - Creates a `pyaudio.PyAudio()` output stream and writes each `chunk` from `response.iter_content(...)` to `stream.write(chunk)` — effectively always playing streaming audio when `args.streaming` is enabled (no `args.play` gate here).
    - Simultaneously writes to a `.wav` file using `wave.open(..., "wb")` and `wf.writeframesraw(chunk)`.

### Relevance to Agent's Goal
- **Where `--play` is defined**: in `parse_args`:
  - `parser.add_argument("--play", type=bool, default=True, help="Whether to play audio after receiving data")`
- **How args are parsed**: `args = parse_args()` in the main block; note that `--play` is parsed as `type=bool`, which in `argparse` commonly leads to surprising behavior (e.g., `--play False` may still evaluate truthy depending on how it’s provided), and there is no `store_true/store_false` pattern.
- **Where playback is triggered**:
  - Non-streaming: `if args.play: play(audio)`
  - Streaming: playback happens unconditionally via `pyaudio` when `args.streaming` is `True` (no check of `args.play`), so `--play` currently does **not** control streaming playback.
- If the PR is about a “API client `--play` flag”, the most relevant code to modify is:
  1. The `--play` argument definition in `parse_args` (its `type=bool` behavior).
  2. The streaming branch to optionally respect `args.play` (or to split “play” vs “save” concerns).

output.pr_title: fix: no-play argument for API client
output.pr_body:
Fix BUG.

Currently, `--play` has no effect because its default value is `True`. The parser does not recognize anything like `--play False`. It only detect whether the argument exists.

Therefore, we can use BooleanOptionalAction, which automatically add the `--no-play` option, so that the play argument can be set to `False`.

Compatibility: This change does not break the existing behaviors because it adds a new option.
output.masca_optimized:
### MASCA (Optimized for PR: add `--no-play` for API client)

#### **M — Motivation / Problem**
`tools/api_client.py` defines `--play` as `type=bool` with `default=True`. With `argparse`, boolean flags should not be parsed via `type=bool` because CLI strings like `"False"` are still truthy when cast (and typical usage expects presence/absence semantics). As a result, users cannot reliably disable playback, and the CLI offers no `--no-play` switch.

Additionally, in *streaming* mode the client plays audio unconditionally via `pyaudio` and does not consult `args.play`, so even a correct `--no-play` would not suppress streaming playback unless that branch is also gated.

#### **A — Affected Areas**
- CLI parsing for `--play` in `tools/api_client.py:parse_args`.
- Playback behavior:
  - Non-streaming path uses `if args.play: pydub.playback.play(audio)`.
  - Streaming path writes chunks to a `pyaudio` output stream unconditionally.

#### **S — Suggested Solution / Design**
- Replace the current `--play` argument with `argparse.BooleanOptionalAction`:
  - `parser.add_argument('--play', action=argparse.BooleanOptionalAction, default=True, ...)`
  - This automatically provides both `--play` and `--no-play`.
- Ensure `args.play` controls *all* playback:
  - Non-streaming: keep `if args.play: ...`
  - Streaming: only initialize/use `pyaudio` output when `args.play` is `True`; still allow saving to wav regardless.

#### **C — Compatibility / Risks**
- Backward compatible: `--play` still exists and defaults to `True`.
- New behavior: `--no-play` becomes available.
- Minor behavioral clarification needed: in streaming mode, `--no-play` should suppress local playback but still save the stream to file.

#### **A — Acceptance Criteria**
- `python tools/api_client.py ... --no-play` results in no local audio output.
- `python tools/api_client.py ... --play` still plays audio.
- Default behavior (no flag) continues playing audio.
- Behavior is consistent in both streaming and non-streaming modes.

#### **Testing Notes**
- Manual CLI checks:
  - `--no-play` with `--streaming` and without `--streaming`.
  - Verify file output is still written in both modes.

---

files_to_modify.reason: Contains the argparse definition of `--play` (currently `type=bool, default=True`) and the actual playback logic in both streaming and non-streaming branches; must be updated to use `BooleanOptionalAction` and to ensure streaming playback respects `args.play`.

functions_to_modify.reason: Defines `--play`; should be changed from `type=bool` to `action=argparse.BooleanOptionalAction` (default True) to auto-enable `--no-play` and make CLI semantics correct.

output.analysis_summary: The bug originates in `tools/api_client.py` where `--play` is incorrectly implemented as `type=bool` with a default of `True`, preventing users from disabling playback via CLI. The PR should update `parse_args` to use `argparse.BooleanOptionalAction` so `--no-play` is available. While exploring the file, streaming playback was found to bypass `args.play` entirely (unconditional `pyaudio` output), so to make `--no-play` truly effective, the streaming branch should also conditionally initialize/use the playback stream based on `args.play`.

agents.name: context_planner_agent
agents.system_prompt:
You are a Context Planner Agent specialized in step plan generation.

## Your Task
You receive from the Analysis Agent:
- PR title and body
- Optimized MASCA
- List of files to modify
- List of functions to modify

You must:
1. **Retrieve context**: Use tools to read the context files of the identified functions
2. **Analyze dependencies**: Understand how the functions interact
3. **Generate step plan**: Create a detailed step-by-step plan

## Available Tools
- `read_context_file(file_path)`: Read a pre-generated context file
- `list_context_files(directory, pattern)`: List available context files
- `read_call_graph(section)`: Read information from the call graph

## Required Output
You must produce a `step_plan` with:
- `summary`: 1-2 sentence summary of what the PR does
- `steps`: Ordered list of steps, each with:
  - `operation`: What to do (e.g., "Add validation method")
  - `file_to_modify`: Relative path of the file to modify (e.g., "src/module.py")
  - `function_to_modify`: Name of the function to modify (e.g., "ClassName.method_name")
  - `reason`: Technical motivation
  - `side_effects`: Potential impacts

## IMPORTANT RULE for function_to_modify
- If the file to modify is a Python file (.py), specify the function/method to modify
- If the file is NOT a Python file (e.g., .js, .html, .css, .json, .yaml, .md, etc.),
  the value of `function_to_modify` MUST be null

## Guidelines for Steps
- Each step must be atomic and implementable
- The order of steps must be logical (dependencies first)
- For Python files: always specify the function in the format "FunctionName" or "ClassName.method_name"
- For non-Python files: function_to_modify must be null
- The side_effects should mention tests, dependents, and API impacts
- You MUST generate a number of step EQUAL to the number of the python functions you are intendeed to modify nomore neither less

## Step Examples

### For Python files:
```json
{
  "operation": "Add time_spanned_alpha method",
  "file_to_modify": "manimlib/animation/animation.py",
  "function_to_modify": "Animation.time_spanned_alpha",
  "reason": "To compute animation progress based on actual elapsed time",
  "side_effects": "May require downstream animations to call this method"
}
```

### For non-Python files:
```json
{
  "operation": "Update API endpoint configuration",
  "file_to_modify": "config/routes.yaml",
  "function_to_modify": null,
  "reason": "Add new route for the authentication endpoint",
  "side_effects": "Requires server restart to apply changes"
}
```
agents.input_prompt:
Generate an implementation plan for this PR:

## PR Title
fix: no-play argument for API client

## PR Body
Fix BUG.

Currently, `--play` has no effect because its default value is `True`. The parser does not recognize anything like `--play False`. It only detect whether the argument exists.

Therefore, we can use BooleanOptionalAction, which automatically add the `--no-play` option, so that the play argument can be set to `False`.

Compatibility: This change does not break the existing behaviors because it adds a new option.

## MASCA Analysis (optimized)
### MASCA (Optimized for PR: add `--no-play` for API client)

#### **M — Motivation / Problem**
`tools/api_client.py` defines `--play` as `type=bool` with `default=True`. With `argparse`, boolean flags should not be parsed via `type=bool` because CLI strings like `"False"` are still truthy when cast (and typical usage expects presence/absence semantics). As a result, users cannot reliably disable playback, and the CLI offers no `--no-play` switch.

Additionally, in *streaming* mode the client plays audio unconditionally via `pyaudio` and does not consult `args.play`, so even a correct `--no-play` would not suppress streaming playback unless that branch is also gated.

#### **A — Affected Areas**
- CLI parsing for `--play` in `tools/api_client.py:parse_args`.
- Playback behavior:
  - Non-streaming path uses `if args.play: pydub.playback.play(audio)`.
  - Streaming path writes chunks to a `pyaudio` output stream unconditionally.

#### **S — Suggested Solution / Design**
- Replace the current `--play` argument with `argparse.BooleanOptionalAction`:
  - `parser.add_argument('--play', action=argparse.BooleanOptionalAction, default=True, ...)`
  - This automatically provides both `--play` and `--no-play`.
- Ensure `args.play` controls *all* playback:
  - Non-streaming: keep `if args.play: ...`
  - Streaming: only initialize/use `pyaudio` output when `args.play` is `True`; still allow saving to wav regardless.

#### **C — Compatibility / Risks**
- Backward compatible: `--play` still exists and defaults to `True`.
- New behavior: `--no-play` becomes available.
- Minor behavioral clarification needed: in streaming mode, `--no-play` should suppress local playback but still save the stream to file.

#### **A — Acceptance Criteria**
- `python tools/api_client.py ... --no-play` results in no local audio output.
- `python tools/api_client.py ... --play` still plays audio.
- Default behavior (no flag) continues playing audio.
- Behavior is consistent in both streaming and non-streaming modes.

#### **Testing Notes**
- Manual CLI checks:
  - `--no-play` with `--streaming` and without `--streaming`.
  - Verify file output is still written in both modes.

---


## Files to Modify
- tools/api_client.py: Contains the argparse definition of `--play` (currently `type=bool, default=True`) and the actual playback logic in both streaming and non-streaming branches; must be updated to use `BooleanOptionalAction` and to ensure streaming playback respects `args.play`.

## Functions to Modify
- parse_args (tools/api_client.py): Defines `--play`; should be changed from `type=bool` to `action=argparse.BooleanOptionalAction` (default True) to auto-enable `--no-play` and make CLI semantics correct.

## Analysis Summary
The bug originates in `tools/api_client.py` where `--play` is incorrectly implemented as `type=bool` with a default of `True`, preventing users from disabling playback via CLI. The PR should update `parse_args` to use `argparse.BooleanOptionalAction` so `--no-play` is available. While exploring the file, streaming playback was found to bypass `args.play` entirely (unconditional `pyaudio` output), so to make `--no-play` truly effective, the streaming branch should also conditionally initialize/use the playback stream based on `args.play`.

Use the context files to understand dependencies and generate a detailed step-by-step plan.

steps.operation: Fix CLI boolean parsing for playback by switching --play to BooleanOptionalAction (enabling --no-play) and ensure the parsed value remains default-True for backward compatibility
steps.reason: argparse does not handle `type=bool` as users expect for flags; using `argparse.BooleanOptionalAction` provides correct presence/absence semantics and automatically adds `--no-play` while keeping existing `--play` behavior and default playback enabled.
steps.side_effects: Users gain a new `--no-play` flag; existing invocations without `--play` continue to play by default. If callers previously attempted `--play False`, behavior will change to the correct interpretation (the recommended usage becomes `--no-play`). Requires Python 3.9+ (BooleanOptionalAction) or a compatibility fallback if the project supports older versions; manual CLI verification should cover both streaming and non-streaming modes to confirm playback suppression works end-to-end.

step_plan.summary: Add proper boolean flag handling for audio playback in the API client by replacing the broken `--play type=bool` argument with `BooleanOptionalAction`, which introduces `--no-play` while preserving default behavior.
